beats.title: KNOW YOUR REALM
beats.subtitle: Security Categorization · FIPS 199
say: Before you pick defenses, you must know what you're defending, and how much it matters.
This is categorization, and it begins with a federal standard: FIPS 199.

beats.plain: FIPS are mandatory U.S. federal standards. To 'categorize' a system is to rate how serious the harm would be — Low, Moderate, or High — if its confidentiality, integrity, or availability were lost.
say: FIPS — Federal Information Processing Standards, the mandatory ones. Categorizing is sizing the stakes for C, I, and A — the triad from Episode zero.

beats.title: Three security objectives
say: FIPS 199 judges a system on three objectives.
Confidentiality, Integrity, and Availability. The C-I-A triad.

beats.title: Rate each at Low, Moderate, or High
bullets: Confidentiality — harm if data is disclosed
Integrity — harm if data is altered or destroyed
Availability — harm if access is disrupted
Each gets an impact level: Low, Moderate, or High
say: For each objective, ask: how bad is the worst-case impact?

beats.title: The high-water mark
bullets: The system's overall category = the HIGHEST of the three
Example: Confidentiality Moderate, Integrity Low, Availability Moderate
One High anywhere makes the whole system High
say: So one High pulls the whole system up?
Exactly. We call it the high-water mark. The worst case wins.

beats.title: Two more companions
bullets: SP 800-60 — maps your information TYPES to impact levels
FIPS 200 — the minimum security requirements for federal systems
FIPS 200 is what mandates using SP 800-53 in the first place
say: 800-60 helps you rate each information type.
And FIPS 200 sets the floor, and points you to 800-53.

beats.title: Security Categorization
beats.plain: Categorize the system and its information, and document the results.
beats.why: Categorization is itself a control, RA-2, the control behind the RMF Categorize step.
say: And the catalog has a control for exactly this: RA-2.
Categorize the system and information it processes, stores, and transmits;

beats.q: A system is Confidentiality=Moderate, Integrity=Low, Availability=High. Its category is?
say: Scenario time. What's the overall category? Pause, and answer.

beats.q: Which standard defines the Low / Moderate / High impact levels?
say: And which standard sets the impact levels themselves?

beats.title: Episode 09 — remember this
bullets: FIPS 199 rates Confidentiality, Integrity, Availability as Low / Moderate / High
Overall category = the high-water mark (the highest of the three)
800-60 maps information types; FIPS 200 sets the floor and points to 800-53; RA-2 documents it
say: We know the realm's worth. Now we choose its defenses.
Next: the armory, and the baselines of 800-53B.
